threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Clapton Pond instead of just 'the pond.' Locals have a shorthand for everything, even the tiny green spaces.
Heard a guy on the 59 last night swear he saw a fox wearing a collar. The bus driver just nodded, like this is normal.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found my favorite baker on the corner who remembers my name. It's the little things that make you feel at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a café that serves kebabs with organic vegan options and espresso – all under one roof and at 2 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the El Metate truck is the real late-night savior, not those trendy taco spots everyone raves about.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a sax on the 16 line, sax in one hand, coffee in the other. Only in SF do we make multi-tasking an art form.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the 'poble' in Gràcia is about the village vibe, but it actually means 'town'—we're not just a pretty face, there's real history here.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their reluctance to step off the sidewalk for street performers—locals know it’s just part of the scene.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'local' means the pub on your corner, not the corner shop where you buy your dinner.
Caught a whiff of fried plantains last night; turns out the guy in the back had a whole stash—just him and a boiling hot Tupperware.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a grandma arguing with a pigeon over a piece of bread on the U7 platform, I realized Neukölln had a magic all its own.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a bakery that serves fresh pretzels alongside vegan kebabs at 2 AM. It's a weirdly perfect pairing.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their willingness to wait for the 14-Mission instead of hopping into a rideshare at the first sign of rain.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw someone serenading the 14 bus line regulars with a guitar, while a pigeon tried to steal their sandwich. Classic Mission vibes.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the good tapas spots are in the guidebooks, but the best ones are tucked away in the side streets, often with no signs at all.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still stand confused at the tram stops, watching the schedules like it's a foreign language. Meanwhile, the locals just flow with it.
